title: AI-SDLC MetricSnapshot v1
description: Operator-supplied journey success-metric snapshot resource per RFC-0018 §10.1 OQ-5. Operators push MetricSnapshot records from their analytics pipeline (Mixpanel, Amplitude, Heap, internal) to supply completion-rate and other journey-success signals. The framework reads these values for Cκ scoring at journey scope — it does not compute them from an analytics backend.

metadata.description: Metadata identifying the journey and metric this snapshot belongs to.

journey.description: Path-style journey URI. Soul-scoped: '<soul-id>/<journey-id>'. Variant-scoped: '<soul-id>/<variant-id>/<journey-id>'. Mirrors the targetedJourneys URI format from RFC-0018 §5.4.

metricId.description: Metric identifier. Kebab-case. MUST match an id in the parent journey's successMetrics[] array. Examples: 'completion-rate', 'median-time-to-first-task-done'.

labels.description: Optional key/value labels for grouping, filtering, or operator tagging.

annotations.description: Optional key/value annotations (freeform, not used by the framework for scoring).

spec.description: The metric snapshot value and provenance.

value.description: Metric value. Dimensionless — the caller and schema consumer share the same unit convention declared on the parent journey's successMetrics[].id. Examples: 0.65 for completion-rate (ratio), 1800 for time-based metrics (seconds). The framework does not validate dimensional consistency.

recordedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this metric value was recorded / sampled by the analytics source. Used to compute staleness (default 30d, per-Soul configurable via journey.successMetrics.staleness.thresholdDays).

sourceTool.description: Free-text identifier for the analytics tool that produced this metric. Examples: 'mixpanel', 'amplitude', 'heap', 'internal-pipeline', 'dbt-metrics', 'custom-warehouse-query'. Not validated against an allowlist — adopter-supplied tooling names are heterogeneous.

windowStart.description: Optional ISO 8601 start of the measurement window (e.g. rolling 7-day window start). Informational — not used in staleness computation.

windowEnd.description: Optional ISO 8601 end of the measurement window. When present, SHOULD equal or precede recordedAt. Informational — not used in staleness computation.
